phase_full_name: Phase 31CF-R — llama.cpp GGUF FFN-Input Hook-Point Diagnosis / Patch Design
classification_rationale: 31CF-R produced a design that identifies a recommended hook (Candidate E) with strong source-analysis support. The recommendation is well-grounded in source inspection (4 files / ~250 lines) and binary-level symbol verification (operator's local llama-server contains no PRT sidecar symbols). However, the design has not been runtime-verified; Candidate E's correctness is design-stage, not runtime-verified. Therefore the classification is PARTIAL (candidates identified + recommended strategy) rather than PASS (design + runtime-verified).

files_lines_inspected.src/llama-graph.cpp: 1295-1303 (llm_graph_context::cb), 1342-1366 (build_prt_true_attn_out_injection), 1450-1541 (build_prt_true_ffn_up_injection), 1553-1648 (build_prt_true_ffn_gate_injection), 1660-1770 (build_prt_true_ffn_down_injection), 1844-1933 (build_ffn signature + entry + first PRT sidecar block), 27/1346/1455 (#ifdef PRT_SIDECAR_PAGER_EXPERIMENTAL guards), 100-112 (counter globals), 30/51/57/213/226/236/242/248/254/260 (PRT env-var reads)

binary_symbol_check.interpretation: Operator's local llama-server was built WITHOUT -DPRT_SIDECAR_PAGER_EXPERIMENTAL. The dormant PRT sidecar is inactive at the binary level.

A.name: dump cur to disk at build_ffn entry
A.expected_shape: [N_tokens, 1536] for Qwen2.5-1.5B at L0 (post-norm, shape matches 31CD but values differ)
A.validation_risk: MEDIUM (post-norm shape-identity mismatch with 31CD pre-norm capture)

B.name: use sidecar's prt_get_residual_view(il, 'ffn_up') as the hook
B.tensor_captured: decoded residual view R (NOT activation X)
B.single_layer_token_writable: theoretically yes, but tied to sidecar apply-layer gating
B.validation_risk: HIGH (different tensor type, cannot cross-validate against 31CD)

C.name: add a cb() callback before build_ffn in qwen.cpp (at line 76, the cur = build_ffn(...) call site)
C.file_function_lines: ~/llama.cpp/src/models/qwen.cpp line 76 (between line 74 cb(cur, 'ffn_norm', il) and the build_ffn call)
C.tensor_captured: post-norm cur (functionally identical to Candidate A's entry argument)
C.sidecar_interference_risk: LOW-MEDIUM (call site is in qwen.cpp, not inside build_ffn)
C.validation_risk: MEDIUM (post-norm shape-identity mismatch with 31CD pre-norm capture)
C.recommendation: MAYBE / REJECT as primary (post-norm issue is the same as Candidate A)

D.name: diagnostic callback / named tensor capture after ffn_norm and before ffn_up/ffn_gate matmuls, via ggml graph callback (hypothetical cb(cur, 'ffn_norm_post', il))
D.file_function_lines: hypothetical insertion between qwen.cpp:74 and qwen.cpp:76, or new ggml-level callback mechanism
D.tensor_captured: post-norm cur (functionally identical to Candidate C)
D.recommendation: MAYBE / equivalent to Candidate C (no advantage over C)

E.name: use the existing cb(ffn_inp, 'ffn_inp', il) callback at qwen.cpp:67 (already in source, no patch needed for the callback itself)
E.tensor_captured: pre-norm ffn_inp (post-attention-residual-add hidden state at the layer boundary)
E.sidecar_interference_risk: LOW (callback fires outside build_ffn; sidecar is inside build_ffn and inside build_attn via build_prt_true_attn_out_injection)
E.gating_cleanliness: YES (uses existing cb_func observer mechanism, default-off via env-var)
E.expected_shape: [1, 1536] for Qwen2.5-1.5B at L0 with 'The capital of France is' (6 tokens), last prefill token (same shape as 31CD's capture, contextual comparison only, not bit-equal)
E.implementation_risk: LOW (callback already exists; patch is a new cb_func filter + post-llama_decode serialize step)
E.validation_risk: LOW (captured tensor is at the same semantic tensor position as 31CD, but the comparison to 31CD's HF forward-hook capture is contextual, not bit-equal, and not an equivalence claim: 31CD used HF-derived activation proxies; a future 31CF-S would capture exact Q4_K_M GGUF runtime activations. Validation asserts same sign pattern + same order of magnitude + per-element absolute differences in [1e-3, 1e-1] as contextual indicators, not as a claim of value identity or Option A equals Option B)

recommended_strategy.shape: [1, 1536] for Qwen2.5-1.5B at L0 with 'The capital of France is', batch=1, last prefill token (same shape as 31CD's capture)
recommended_strategy.why_recommended: The ONLY candidate that captures the same semantic tensor position as 31CD/31CE — the pre-FFN input activation X entering the selected layer MLP — but it does NOT imply identical activation values. 31CD/31CE used HF-derived activation proxies (HF safetensors bf16 forward-hook on mdl.model.layers[il].mlp); a future 31CF-S would capture exact Q4_K_M GGUF runtime activations via llama.cpp instrumentation. The two captures are at the same semantic tensor position but are numerically different methods; the comparison is contextual only, not an equivalence claim. Low sidecar risk (callback fires outside build_ffn; build-gate eliminates sidecar). Clean gating (existing cb_func observer mechanism, no new public API, default-off via env-var). 31CF missed this candidate because they focused on the cur at build_ffn entry (Candidate A) and the sidecar machinery inside build_ffn; they did not evaluate the already-existing cb(ffn_inp, 'ffn_inp', il) at qwen.cpp:67.

compile_time_gates.name: SDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED
compile_time_gates.purpose: Master switch; off -> no SDI capture code compiled in, zero runtime cost

compile_time_gates.name: PRT_SIDECAR_PAGER_EXPERIMENTAL (build requirement)
compile_time_gates.purpose: Build MUST NOT define this; if it does, SDI capture is auto-disabled with a one-time stderr warning

runtime_env_var_gates.name: SDI_CAPTURE_FFN_INPUT
runtime_env_var_gates.purpose: Master runtime switch for ffn_inp capture path. Unset -> no capture.

runtime_env_var_gates.name: SDI_CAPTURE_LAYER
runtime_env_var_gates.purpose: Layer id to capture. Unset -> no capture.

runtime_env_var_gates.name: SDI_CAPTURE_TOKEN
runtime_env_var_gates.purpose: Token position to capture. last = last prefill token (matches 31CD position). Numeric = specific token index. all = entire prefill sequence (off by default).

runtime_env_var_gates.name: SDI_CAPTURE_OUT
runtime_env_var_gates.purpose: Output path. Unset -> no capture. Opened O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC|O_NOFOLLOW, written atomically, mode 0600.

runtime_env_var_gates.name: SDI_CAPTURE_EXIT_AFTER
runtime_env_var_gates.purpose: If set, exit the process after the capture write completes. Default unset -> continue (for use with llama-server for HTTP-driven capture).

default_off_guarantees: All gates default to off. Unset state of any required gate -> no capture, no file write, zero I/O.
cb_func registration is always active (passive observer; cost is one pointer-dereference per cb() call, negligible). The env-var gates determine whether the observer records anything.
No all-layer dumping by default: SDI_CAPTURE_LAYER must be set explicitly, and the observer only records the single layer.
No all-token dumping by default: SDI_CAPTURE_TOKEN=*** (or numeric) is the default; SDI_CAPTURE_TOKEN=*** requires explicit opt-in and is intended for diagnostic use only.
No raw activation arrays in repo: writes go to SDI_CAPTURE_OUT (operator-controlled, e.g. /tmp/...); no SDI repo path receives raw arrays.

header_layout.value: index of captured token in prefill (e.g. 5 for last of 6)
first 4 bytes of SHA256 of prompt bytes (for traceability; no PII)

steps.name: build_confirmation
steps.requirement: Confirm ~/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server is operator's local build (not atomic chat's). Confirm nm shows no PRT sidecar symbols AND no SDI capture symbols (clean default build). Confirm CMake invocation does not include -DPRT_SIDECAR_PAGER_EXPERIMENTAL and does not include -DSDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED=ON.

steps.name: hook_disabled_by_default
steps.requirement: Run llama-server with no SDI_* env vars. Verify via strace that no SDIX files are written. Stop if any capture occurs.

steps.name: hook_enabled_only_for_selected_layer_token
steps.requirement: Set SDI_CAPTURE_FFN_INPUT=1, SDI_CAPTURE_LAYER=0, SDI_CAPTURE_TOKEN=*** SDI_CAPTURE_OUT=/tmp/phase31cfs_p0_l0.bin. Run a single prefill of 'The capital of France is' at L0 with batch=1. Verify output file: magic=SDIX, version=1, dtype=float32, n_dim=2, dim[0]=1, dim[1]=1536, token_position=5, il=0, N=1536 float32 values.

steps.name: activation_finite_check
steps.requirement: Read 1536 float32 values; verify np.isfinite(X).all() == True. If any non-finite value, stop and report.

steps.name: sha256_of_raw_X
steps.requirement: Compute sha256(X.tobytes()) and record in result JSON.

steps.name: replay_through_31cd_pipeline
steps.requirement: Replay captured X through the 31CD standalone pipeline: W_ref = local 1.5B Q4_K_M GGUF dequantized, W_low = corrected_ceil_per_row Q2_K, SDIR = ffn_up + ffn_gate k=0.5%, no ffn_down residual. Compute metrics: cos_low, cos_sub, delta_cos, MAE_low, MAE_sub, MAE_delta, finite, severe, memory margin.

steps.name: comparison_to_31cd_31ce_p0_l0_context_only
steps.requirement: 31CD's HF forward-hook capture is at the same semantic tensor position (input to the L0 MLP, pre-norm, shape [1, 1536]) but is NOT bit-equal to a GGUF Q4_K_M capture. Expected: same sign pattern, same order of magnitude, per-element abs diff in [1e-3, 1e-1] as contextual indicators. Validation is contextual only, NOT a pass/fail gate, NOT an equivalence claim, NOT a claim that Option A equals Option B.

steps.name: no_claim_of_option_a_hf_equivalence
steps.requirement: 31CF-R and 31CF-S do not claim Option A (HF bf16) and Option B (GGUF Q4_K_M) produce identical activation values. 31CC explicitly distinguishes them. 31CF-S is a NEW ground truth, not a replication.

steps.name: no_raw_activation_arrays_committed
steps.requirement: Capture file written to SDI_CAPTURE_OUT (operator-controlled). Never committed to sdi-substitutive repo. Result JSON records sha256, captured_shape, dtype, token_position, il, and replay metrics only.

steps.name: post_edit_regression
steps.requirement: After any future 31CF-S implementation, run python3 -m tests.run_source_of_truth_regression again. Must remain PASS_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH_RUNTIME_CLEAN. If fails, stop and report.

files_that_would_be_modified_in_future_3.modification: Add option(SDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED 'Enable SDI activation capture (default OFF)' OFF)
Declare sdi_capture_register_cb_func(), sdi_capture_get_recorded_tensor(), sdi_capture_serialize_if_ready(). Guarded by #ifdef SDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED.
Implement the registered cb_func, the env-var gates, the ggml_get_data read, the SDIX file write. Guarded by #ifdef SDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED. ~80 lines.
Call sdi_capture_register_cb_func() once at server startup. Guarded by #ifdef SDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED.

no_edits_to: qwen.cpp (no source change needed; cb(ffn_inp, 'ffn_inp', il) already exists at line 67)
llama-graph.cpp (no source change needed; llm_graph_context::cb already exists at line 1299)
build_ffn (no source change needed; the sidecar machinery is dormant at the binary level and the candidate-E hook fires outside build_ffn)
patch_outline.atomic_write: write() to temp file + fsync() + rename() to final path. Permissions 0600.
patch_outline.disable_by_default: SDI_CAPTURE_ENABLED=OFF in CMake (default) -> sdi_capture.cpp/h not compiled. Even with ON, env-var gates all unset -> cb_func filter records nothing, serialize short-circuits on missing gates.

agents_md_summary.ai_agent_proceed_only_if_contributor_can: relevant for future 31CF-S; 31CF-R is documentation, not code

allowed_claims: A design for a llama.cpp GGUF FFN-input hook has been produced and reviewed in this document
The recommended hook (Candidate E, cb(ffn_inp, 'ffn_inp', il) at qwen.cpp:67) is already in source and requires no source patch to the existing callback
The dormant PRT sidecar machinery is inactive at the binary level in the operator's current ~/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server (verified via strings | grep returning 0 PRT symbols)

The recommended hook captures the same semantic tensor position as 31CD/31CE (the pre-FFN input activation X entering the selected layer MLP, shape [1, 1536] for Qwen2.5-1.5B at L0 with 'The capital of France is'). It does NOT imply that 31CF-S would capture identical activation values to 31CD/31CE's HF forward-hook: 31CD/31CE used HF-derived activation proxies; a future 31CF-S would capture exact Q4_K_M GGUF runtime activations. The two methods target the same semantic tensor position but are numerically different; the comparison is contextual only, not an equivalence claim.

A future 31CF-S implementation can be gated at three levels: CMake option (default OFF), env vars (all unset by default), and the existing cb_func callback filter (which records nothing unless the gates are satisfied)
forbidden_claims: no exact activation capture claim (no capture has been performed in 31CF-R)
no replay metric claim (no replay has been performed in 31CF-R)
no hook implementation claim (the design proposes a hook but no implementation exists in 31CF-R)
no build success claim (no build was performed or attempted in 31CF-R)
no runtime integration claim (no runtime integration exists in 31CF-R)
no generation quality claim (out of scope)
no production readiness claim (out of scope)
no claim that a candidate hook is correct unless proven by source analysis (Candidate E correctness is design-stage, supported by source inspection, not runtime-verified)

no claim that Option A HF activations equal Option B GGUF activations (31CC explicitly distinguishes them; 31CF-R design proposal makes clear that 31CD/31CE used HF-derived activation proxies and a future 31CF-S would use exact Q4_K_M GGUF runtime activations; these are not bit-equal and the comparison is contextual only)
no model files / raw activation arrays / build artifacts committed
no commit, push, tag without explicit Matt approval (31CF-R stops at PRE-COMMIT REPORT)
no model quality / behavior / speed / runtime / inference / production claim (SOT 0.A items 1-10)
limitations: Candidate E correctness is design-stage, not runtime-verified. A future 31CF-S implementation must build, run, capture, and validate before any claim of 'the hook works at runtime' can be made.

Cross-validation against 31CD/31CE is contextual only (same semantic tensor position, not bit-equal, not an equivalence claim). HF safetensors bf16 forward-hook and Q4_K_M GGUF instrumentation are numerically different methods; same-sign-pattern is the most that can be claimed.

The hook captures a single layer/token per process lifetime. Multi-layer / multi-token sweeps require multiple process invocations with different SDI_CAPTURE_LAYER / SDI_CAPTURE_TOKEN values. This is by design (single-shot capture prevents accidental bulk dumps).
The operator's ~/llama.cpp/ binary must be built without -DPRT_SIDECAR_PAGER_EXPERIMENTAL for the design to be valid. If a future build adds this flag, the SDI capture is auto-disabled.

The 31CF blocker (BLOCKED_31CF_HOOK_POINT_AMBIGUOUS) is NOT reclassified by this design. 31CF-R produces a design that would unblock a future 31CF-S implementation, but the BLOCKED classification remains until 31CF-S (or any subsequent implementation phase) actually performs a capture and validates it.
